county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Taylor County formed 1875 from Clark, Lincoln, Marathon, and Chippewa counties. County seat is Medford. Northern Wisconsin lumber county. Strong German settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Taylor County Register of Deeds in Medford holds land records from 1875. Created from Clark, Lincoln, Marathon, and Chippewa counties -- check all four parent counties for pre-1875 records. Northern Wisconsin lumber county. German immigrants were the primary settler group. The Taylor County Historical Society in Medford maintains collections. WHS has microfilmed Taylor County records. Jump River area. Lumber company employment records at WHS. Catholic and Lutheran church records supplement civil records. FamilySearch has limited Taylor County records. BLM GLO patents.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Taylor County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Medford; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Taylor County from 1875; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages and liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records. WHS has microfilm of historical deed books
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1875; no major record losses

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents (preemption, homestead, cash entry, military bounty) for Taylor County; Wisconsin is a public land state and GLO patents document original federal land transfers to private ownership

source_profiles.repository: Taylor County Probate Court, Medford
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, inventories, guardianships, and estate settlements for Taylor County from 1875; Probate Court has jurisdiction over estate matters; WHS has microfilm of historical probate records

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Trempealeau County formed 1854 from Jackson (unorganized territory). County seat is Whitehall. Western Wisconsin county. Very strong Norwegian immigrant settlement -- one of the highest concentrations of Norwegian-born in Wisconsin.
county_metadata.research_tips: Trempealeau County Register of Deeds in Whitehall holds land records from 1854. Named for the French term 'la montagne qui trempe a l'eau' (the mountain that soaks in the water). Western Wisconsin county with VERY strong Norwegian immigrant settlement -- Trempealeau County had one of the highest proportions of Norwegian-born residents in Wisconsin. The Trempealeau County Historical Society in Whitehall maintains collections. WHS has microfilmed Trempealeau County records. Norwegian-American Historical Association (NAHA) at St. Olaf College has emigration records. Whitehall and Pigeon Falls area Norwegian Lutheran churches have extensive records. FamilySearch has some Trempealeau County records. BLM GLO patents.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Trempealeau County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Whitehall; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1854; no major record losses

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Vernon County formed 1851 from Crawford County (originally Bad Ax County, renamed 1862). County seat is Viroqua. Southwestern Wisconsin driftless area county. Norwegian, German, and Czech settlement. Kickapoo Valley.
county_metadata.research_tips: Vernon County Register of Deeds in Viroqua holds land records from 1851. Originally named Bad Ax County (renamed Vernon 1862 after the Battle of Bad Axe during the Black Hawk War became unfashionable). Southwestern Wisconsin driftless area county. Norwegian, German, and Bohemian (Czech) immigrant settlement. The Vernon County Historical Society in Viroqua maintains collections. WHS has microfilmed Vernon County records. Kickapoo Valley Reserve. Organic farming community (Viroqua). FamilySearch has some Vernon County records. BLM GLO patents. Norwegian Lutheran and Catholic church records supplement civil records. Black Hawk War records at NARA and WHS.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Vernon County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Viroqua; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1851; no major record losses

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Vilas County formed 1893 from Oneida County. County seat is Eagle River. Northern Wisconsin Northwoods county with over 1,300 lakes. Lac du Flambeau Band of Lake Superior Chippewa reservation. Tourism and logging heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: Vilas County Register of Deeds in Eagle River holds land records from 1893. Created from Oneida County -- check Oneida County for pre-1893 records. Northern Wisconsin Northwoods county with over 1,300 lakes. Tourism and logging economy. The Lac du Flambeau Band of Lake Superior Chippewa reservation is in Vilas County -- tribal records at NARA Great Lakes Region. The Vilas County Historical Museum in Sayner maintains collections. WHS has microfilmed Vilas County records. Snowmobile Museum in Sayner (Carl Eliason). FamilySearch has limited Vilas County records. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Vilas County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Eagle River; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1893; no major record losses

WHS also holds some Native American records

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Walworth County formed 1836 from Milwaukee County. County seat is Elkhorn. One of Wisconsin's original counties in the southern tier. Lake Geneva resort area. Yankee, Irish, and German settlement. Circus heritage at Delavan.
county_metadata.research_tips: Walworth County Register of Deeds in Elkhorn holds land records from 1836. One of Wisconsin's original counties in the southern tier. Lake Geneva resort area attracted wealthy Chicago families from the 1870s (estate and property records). Yankee, Irish, and German settlement. The Walworth County Historical Society in Elkhorn maintains collections. Webster House Museum in Elkhorn. WHS has microfilmed Walworth County records. Baker Manufacturing Company records. Whitewater Normal School (now UW-Whitewater) records. FamilySearch has indexed Walworth County records. BLM GLO patents. Delavan was a center of circus activity before Baraboo.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Walworth County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Elkhorn; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Washburn County formed 1883 from Burnett County. County seat is Shell Lake. Northwestern Wisconsin lake and forest county. Scandinavian, German, and Finnish settlement. Logging and tourism economy.
county_metadata.research_tips: Washburn County Register of Deeds in Shell Lake holds land records from 1883. Created from Burnett County -- check Burnett County for pre-1883 records. Northwestern Wisconsin county with lakes and forests. Scandinavian (Norwegian, Swedish), German, and Finnish settlement. The Washburn County Historical Society Museum in Shell Lake maintains collections. WHS has microfilmed Washburn County records. Logging and tourism economy. Hunt Hill Audubon Sanctuary area. FamilySearch has limited Washburn County records. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries. Lutheran and Catholic church records supplement civil records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Washburn County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Shell Lake; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Washington County formed 1836 from Brown County. County seat is West Bend. One of Wisconsin's original counties. Very strong German (Catholic) community -- one of the highest proportions of German-born in the state. Holy Hill basilica.
county_metadata.research_tips: Washington County Register of Deeds in West Bend holds land records from 1836. One of Wisconsin's original counties. VERY strong German (Catholic) immigrant community -- Washington County had one of the highest proportions of German-born residents in the state. The Washington County Historical Society and Museum in West Bend has extensive collections. WHS has microfilmed Washington County records. Holy Hill (Basilica of the National Shrine of Mary) Catholic pilgrimage site. Hartford Heritage Auto Museum. Catholic parish records (Archdiocese of Milwaukee) are essential -- many parishes had German-language registers into the 1900s. FamilySearch has indexed many Washington County records. BLM GLO patents.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Washington County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in West Bend; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1836; no major record losses

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Waukesha County formed 1846 from Milwaukee County. County seat is Waukesha, formerly famous as a spring water resort. Major Milwaukee suburban county. Carroll University (1846). Yankee, German, and Irish settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Waukesha County Register of Deeds in Waukesha holds land records from 1846. Created from Milwaukee County. Major Milwaukee suburban county with independent archival resources. Waukesha was famous as a spring water resort town (1880s-1900s). The Waukesha County Historical Society and Museum has excellent collections. WHS has microfilmed Waukesha County records. Carroll University (formerly Carroll College, 1846) archives. Yankee, German, and Irish settlement. Waukesha and Brookfield city directories from the 1870s. Waukesha Freeman newspaper archives (1859+). FamilySearch has indexed Waukesha County records. BLM GLO patents. Strong Methodist, Congregationalist, and Catholic church records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Waukesha County Register of Deeds online access; historical deed books from 1846

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1846; no major record losses

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Waupaca County formed 1851 from Brown County. County seat is Waupaca. Central Wisconsin county. Scandinavian (Norwegian, Danish) and German settlement. Chain O'Lakes tourism. New London Polish community.
county_metadata.research_tips: Waupaca County Register of Deeds in Waupaca holds land records from 1851. Central Wisconsin county with strong Scandinavian (Norwegian, Danish) and German settlement. The Waupaca Historical Society in Waupaca maintains collections. Holly History and Genealogy Center has local family files. WHS has microfilmed Waupaca County records. Dane (Norwegian colony) area. New London has significant Polish community. Chain O'Lakes area tourism records. Lutheran and Catholic church records supplement civil records. FamilySearch has some Waupaca County records. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Waupaca County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Waupaca; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Waupaca County Deed Books -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Waupaca County Register of Deeds, Waupaca
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Waupaca County from 1851; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages and liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records. WHS has microfilm of historical deed books
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1851; no major record losses

source_profiles.source_name: Waupaca County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents (preemption, homestead, cash entry, military bounty) for Waupaca County; Wisconsin is a public land state and GLO patents document original federal land transfers to private ownership

source_profiles.source_name: Waupaca County Probate Court Records
source_profiles.repository: Waupaca County Probate Court, Waupaca
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, inventories, guardianships, and estate settlements for Waupaca County from 1851; Probate Court has jurisdiction over estate matters; WHS has microfilm of historical probate records

source_profiles.source_name: Waupaca County Marriage Records -- County Clerk / WHS / FamilySearch
source_profiles.repository: Waupaca County Clerk, Waupaca / Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records for Waupaca County from 1851; County Clerk issued marriage licenses in Wisconsin; WHS holds some county marriage records on microfilm; FamilySearch has indexed Wisconsin marriages; state vital records registration began 1907 (sporadic earlier compliance)

source_profiles.source_name: Waupaca County Federal and State Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry / WHS
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1860-1950 for Waupaca County; Wisconsin state census 1855, 1865, 1875, 1885, 1895, 1905 at WHS (unusually detailed -- include place of birth, occupation, citizenship status); territorial census 1836 and 1842 may include Waupaca County entries

source_profiles.source_name: Waupaca County Norwegian Lutheran Church Records
source_profiles.repository: Local congregations / Norwegian-American Historical Association (NAHA), St. Olaf College / Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Norwegian Lutheran congregation records (baptisms, confirmations, marriages, burials) for Waupaca County; many records in Norwegian; NAHA at St. Olaf College has emigration records and bygdelag (community) records; WHS has some transcribed Norwegian church records; Vesterheim Norwegian-American Museum in Decorah, IA also holds relevant collections

source_profiles.source_name: Waupaca County Cemetery Records -- Find A Grave / WI GenWeb
source_profiles.repository: Find A Grave / Wisconsin GenWeb / Waupaca County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Waupaca County including church cemeteries, municipal cemeteries, and pioneer family burial grounds; Find A Grave has growing coverage; Wisconsin GenWeb and local historical societies have published transcriptions; DAR chapters have documented many Wisconsin cemeteries

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Waushara County formed 1851 from Marquette County. County seat is Wautoma. Central Wisconsin sand and lake county. Yankee, German, and Norwegian settlement. Redgranite quarries and state prison.
county_metadata.research_tips: Waushara County Register of Deeds in Wautoma holds land records from 1851. Created from Marquette County -- check Marquette County for pre-1851 records. Central Wisconsin county with sandy soil and lakes. Yankee, German, and Norwegian immigrant settlement. The Waushara County Historical Society in Wautoma maintains collections. WHS has microfilmed Waushara County records. Redgranite quarries (state prison built 1898). Wild Rose area. FamilySearch has some Waushara County records. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries. Church records (Lutheran, Methodist, Catholic) supplement civil records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Waushara County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Wautoma; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Waushara County Historic Newspapers -- WHS
source_profiles.repository: Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historic newspapers for Waushara County towns at WHS on microfilm and digital; legal notices, obituaries, marriage announcements, estate settlements, and community news; WHS has one of the nation's largest newspaper collections
